compliance_rules.regulation: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — High-Risk Employment AI
compliance_rules.scope: AI systems used to screen, filter, evaluate, or rank candidates for roles where the candidate may be based in or hired into the EU are classified high-risk under Annex III(4). TalentMesh acts as deployer.
compliance_rules.requirement: Ensure human oversight by a competent reviewer on every AI-assisted advancement decision; use the system per provider instructions; monitor operation and retain automatically generated logs for at least 6 months. No fully automated rejection of EU candidates.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Up to EUR 15M or 3% of worldwide annual turnover for breaches of high-risk obligations.

compliance_rules.regulation: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — FRIA, Transparency & Worker Notice
compliance_rules.scope: Deployer-side transparency and impact-assessment duties for high-risk hiring AI affecting EU candidates and EU-based workers/representatives.
compliance_rules.requirement: Complete a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (Art. 27) before first use; inform affected workers and their representatives before deployment (Art. 26(7)); notify EU candidates they are subject to a high-risk AI system and provide a right to explanation of individual decisions (Art. 86).
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Up to EUR 15M or 3% of worldwide annual turnover; EUR 7.5M or 1% for incorrect/misleading information.

compliance_rules.regulation: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — Provider/Vendor Conformity & Logging
compliance_rules.scope: Procurement and ongoing use of third-party AI hiring tools used on EU candidates; verifies the upstream provider's conformity posture.
compliance_rules.requirement: Before onboarding any AI vendor whose output touches EU candidates, obtain evidence of CE marking, conformity assessment, technical documentation (Annex IV), and EU database registration; retain provider instructions and verify input-data relevance/representativeness within deployer control.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Deployer exposure up to EUR 15M or 3% of worldwide annual turnover for use of non-conforming high-risk systems.

compliance_rules.regulation: EU GDPR (Regulation (EU) 2016/679) — Automated Individual Decision-Making (Art. 22)
compliance_rules.scope: Any fully automated screening, ranking, or rejection that produces legal or similarly significant effects on an EU/EEA candidate.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not make solely-automated significant hiring decisions unless a lawful exception applies; provide a human-in-the-loop with the right to intervention, to express a point of view, and to contest the decision; surface meaningful information about the logic and consequences (Arts. 13-15).
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Up to EUR 20M or 4% of worldwide annual turnover (higher tier).

compliance_rules.regulation: EU GDPR — Lawful Basis, Special Categories & DPIA
compliance_rules.scope: All processing of EU candidate personal data within TalentMesh, including profiling and large-scale systematic evaluation.
compliance_rules.requirement: Establish a lawful basis with purpose limitation and data minimization for all candidate data; do not use special-category data (Art. 9) in profiling absent an explicit exception; complete a DPIA (Art. 35) for systematic large-scale candidate profiling before go-live.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Up to EUR 20M or 4% of worldwide annual turnover.

compliance_rules.regulation: NYC Local Law 144 (Automated Employment Decision Tools) — Bias Audit & Disclosure
compliance_rules.scope: Any AEDT used to substantially assist or replace discretionary screening or promotion decisions for positions located in New York City.
compliance_rules.requirement: Complete an independent bias audit within the prior 12 months before use and publish a summary of the most recent audit (impact ratios by sex, race/ethnicity, and intersectional categories) on the company careers website. Block AEDT use for NYC roles if no current audit exists.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: $500 first violation and up to $1,500 per subsequent violation; each day of non-compliant use is a separate violation.

compliance_rules.regulation: NYC Local Law 144 (Automated Employment Decision Tools) — Candidate Notice
compliance_rules.scope: Candidates and employees evaluated by an AEDT for NYC-based positions.
compliance_rules.requirement: Provide at least 10 business days' notice before AEDT use, stating the job qualifications and characteristics the tool assesses, the data collected and its source, and the mechanism to request an alternative selection process or accommodation.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: $500 first violation and up to $1,500 per subsequent violation; each failed notice is a separate violation.

compliance_rules.regulation: Illinois AI Video Interview Act (820 ILCS 42)
compliance_rules.scope: Applicants asked to record a video interview that is analyzed by AI, for positions based in Illinois.
compliance_rules.requirement: Before the interview, notify the applicant that AI may analyze the video, explain in plain language how the AI works and what general characteristics it evaluates, and obtain consent. Limit video sharing to necessary evaluators and delete within 30 days of an applicant's request.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: No fixed penalty schedule; enforced via the Illinois Human Rights Act / Department of Human Rights and data-deletion obligations.

compliance_rules.regulation: Illinois AI Video Interview Act — Demographic Reporting
compliance_rules.scope: Employers that rely solely on AI analysis of video interviews to decide who advances to an in-person interview for Illinois roles.
compliance_rules.requirement: Where AI analysis alone determines in-person advancement, collect and report applicant race/ethnicity demographic data to the Illinois DCEO annually by December 31, and analyze it for disparities.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Enforced via the Illinois Human Rights Act framework; reporting non-compliance is actionable.

compliance_rules.regulation: Illinois Human Rights Act — AI Amendment (HB 3773 / PA 103-0804)
compliance_rules.scope: Use of AI (including generative AI) in recruitment, hiring, promotion, selection for training, discipline, or terms and conditions of employment for Illinois workers.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not use AI that has the effect of discriminating against IHRA-protected classes (disparate impact is codified), and never use ZIP code as a proxy for a protected class. Provide notice to applicants and employees whenever AI is used for a covered employment purpose, in accessible plain language.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: IHRA remedies: actual damages, civil penalties, and attorneys' fees; enforced by the Illinois Department of Human Rights.

compliance_rules.regulation: Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (740 ILCS 14)
compliance_rules.scope: Collection or use of biometric identifiers (face geometry, voiceprints) via AI video or voice interview analysis of Illinois candidates.
compliance_rules.requirement: Provide written notice and obtain a written release before collecting any biometric identifier; maintain a publicly available retention and destruction schedule; never sell or profit from biometric data; store securely and do not disclose without consent.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Private right of action: $1,000 per negligent and $5,000 per intentional/reckless violation, plus attorneys' fees (major class-action exposure).

compliance_rules.regulation: California FEHA — Automated-Decision Systems (ADS) Regulations (2 CCR §§ 11008 et seq.)
compliance_rules.scope: Use of an ADS that makes or facilitates an employment-benefit decision affecting California applicants; covers vendors acting as the employer's agent.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not use an ADS that causes discrimination, including disparate impact, against an FEHA-protected class. Conduct and document recent anti-bias testing and corrective action; treat vendor/agent ADS conduct as employer liability and confirm vendor testing before use.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: FEHA remedies: compensatory and punitive damages, injunctive relief, attorneys' fees, and administrative penalties.

compliance_rules.regulation: California FEHA — ADS Recordkeeping
compliance_rules.scope: Retention of ADS inputs, outputs, and evaluation records for California hiring decisions.
compliance_rules.requirement: Retain ADS-related records — inputs, outputs/scores/rankings, the criteria applied, and testing/evaluation results — for at least 4 years from the decision or record creation.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: FEHA enforcement; missing records undermine the business-necessity defense and invite liability.

compliance_rules.regulation: California CCPA/CPRA — ADMT Regulations (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.100 et seq.)
compliance_rules.scope: Automated decision-making technology applied to California applicant/employee personal information that produces a significant employment decision.
compliance_rules.requirement: Provide pre-use notice of ADMT, honor the right to opt out of certain ADMT, and grant access to information about the logic and outcome of significant decisions; support rights to know, delete, and limit use of personal information.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: CCPA/CPRA administrative fines up to $2,500 per violation and $7,500 per intentional violation, enforced by the CPPA and CA AG.

compliance_rules.regulation: Colorado AI Act — ADMT Framework (SB 24-205 as repealed/replaced by SB 26-189)
compliance_rules.scope: AI/ADMT used in consequential employment decisions affecting Colorado residents. The original high-risk duty-of-care, impact-assessment, and risk-management mandates were eliminated; the replacement disclosure-and-rights ADMT framework governs.
compliance_rules.requirement: Track and implement the SB 26-189 ADMT disclosure-and-rights regime: notify Colorado candidates when AI factors into a consequential decision and provide rights to explanation, correction, and human review. Do not implement the repealed SB 24-205 risk-management program as if in force.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Colorado AG enforcement (no private right of action); violations treated as deceptive trade practices under the CCPA, up to $20,000 per violation.

compliance_rules.regulation: Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (TRAIGA / HB 149)
compliance_rules.scope: AI hiring systems deployed on Texas residents or by an entity doing business in Texas; private-sector discrimination prohibition is intent-based.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not develop or deploy AI with the intent to unlawfully discriminate against a protected class. Adopt and document alignment with a recognized framework (NIST AI RMF) to qualify for the statutory safe harbor; maintain governance evidence and remediate curable violations within the 60-day cure period.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Civil penalties $10,000-$200,000 per violation and $2,000-$40,000 per day for continuing violations; Texas AG exclusive enforcement, no private right of action.

compliance_rules.regulation: Utah Artificial Intelligence Policy Act (UAIP / SB 149)
compliance_rules.scope: Generative-AI chatbots or agents (e.g., AI recruiters, screening chatbots) interacting with Utah candidates, especially high-risk interactions collecting sensitive or biometric data.
compliance_rules.requirement: Clearly and conspicuously disclose that the candidate is interacting with AI rather than a human — proactively and up front for high-risk interactions, and on request in general consumer contexts. Deployer remains liable for AI conduct under Utah consumer-protection law.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Administrative fine up to $2,500 per violation; court/AG civil penalty up to $5,000 per violation of an order.

compliance_rules.regulation: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 — Disparate Impact via AI
compliance_rules.scope: All AI selection procedures (screening, ranking, assessment) used by TalentMesh, which has 15+ employees; covers race, color, religion, sex, and national origin.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not use AI selection tools that cause adverse impact on a protected group unless they are job-related and consistent with business necessity, and adopt any available less-discriminatory alternative. Employer remains responsible for vendor-built tools.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Compensatory and punitive damages capped $50k-$300k per claimant, back pay, injunctive relief, attorneys' fees.

compliance_rules.regulation: EEOC Uniform Guidelines on Employee Selection Procedures — Four-Fifths Rule (29 C.F.R. Part 1607)
compliance_rules.scope: Adverse-impact monitoring of every AI-assisted selection stage (screen, assessment, interview advancement, offer) across protected groups.
compliance_rules.requirement: Continuously compute selection-rate ratios and flag any group whose selection rate falls below 80% (four-fifths) of the highest group's rate; halt or remediate the affected stage and validate job-relatedness before further use.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Underlies Title VII liability; unaddressed adverse impact supports discrimination claims and EEOC enforcement.

compliance_rules.regulation: Americans with Disabilities Act (Title I) — AI Accessibility & Accommodation
compliance_rules.scope: AI assessments, video interviews, and gamified/cognitive tests that could screen out individuals with disabilities or constitute a disability-related inquiry.
compliance_rules.requirement: Offer reasonable accommodations and an accessible alternative to any AI-based assessment on request; ensure tools measure job-related abilities rather than disability-correlated traits; avoid affect/voice/personality analysis that infers disability; notify candidates that accommodations are available.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Title VII-style remedies: compensatory/punitive damages capped by employer size, injunctive relief, attorneys' fees.

compliance_rules.regulation: Americans with Disabilities Act (Title I) — Alternative Process Request
compliance_rules.scope: Candidate-initiated requests for a human-driven or non-AI alternative path through any stage of the hiring process.
compliance_rules.requirement: Provide and honor a documented mechanism for candidates to request an alternative selection process; route such requests to a human reviewer without penalty to the candidate, consistent with the platform's standing 'candidates may request an alternative process' commitment.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: ADA remedies; failure to accommodate is independently actionable.

compliance_rules.regulation: Age Discrimination in Employment Act (29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq.)
compliance_rules.scope: AI sourcing, targeted job-ad delivery, resume parsing, and assessment scoring that may disadvantage applicants age 40+; TalentMesh exceeds the 20-employee threshold.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not use AI features or proxies correlated with age (graduation dates, tenure-based scoring, 'digital native' phrasing) that disadvantage candidates 40+; validate that selection criteria are job-related and monitor age-band outcomes.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Back pay, liquidated (double) damages for willful violations, injunctive relief, attorneys' fees.

compliance_rules.regulation: Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (Title II, 42 U.S.C. § 2000ff et seq.)
compliance_rules.scope: AI sourcing or screening tools that ingest broad public or social-media data which could reveal genetic information or family medical history.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not collect, infer, or use genetic information or family medical history in any hiring decision; restrict web-scraping and social-media analysis tools so they cannot ingest genetic or family-health data.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Title VII-style remedies (damages capped by employer size, injunctive relief, attorneys' fees); EEOC enforcement.

compliance_rules.regulation: OFCCP — Section 503 Rehabilitation Act & VEVRAA (41 C.F.R. Part 60)
compliance_rules.scope: Federal-contractor hiring; OFCCP audits of AI selection procedures for adverse impact, validation, and disability/veteran nondiscrimination. EO 11246 race/sex affirmative-action duties were rescinded by EO 14173; Sec. 503 and VEVRAA remain in force.
compliance_rules.requirement: Maintain adverse-impact analyses and validation records for AI selection tools per UGESP, produce AI tool validity and impact-ratio documentation on audit, and uphold nondiscrimination obligations toward individuals with disabilities and protected veterans.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Loss or debarment of federal contracts, back pay, injunctive relief, conciliation agreements.

compliance_rules.regulation: Maryland Facial Recognition in Employment Interviews (Md. Lab. & Empl. § 3-717 / HB 1202)
compliance_rules.scope: Use of a facial recognition service to create a facial template during an applicant's job interview for Maryland positions.
compliance_rules.requirement: Obtain the applicant's signed written waiver before using facial recognition in an interview; the waiver must include the applicant's name, the interview date, consent to facial-recognition use, and confirmation the applicant read the waiver.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: No standalone penalty schedule; enforceable via general employment-law mechanisms and civil liability.

compliance_rules.regulation: New Jersey Law Against Discrimination — Algorithmic Discrimination (N.J.S.A. 10:5-1 et seq.)
compliance_rules.scope: AI and automated decision tools affecting New Jersey applicants and employees; vendor-built tools included.
compliance_rules.requirement: Do not use AI tools that cause disparate impact or disparate treatment on LAD-protected classes; perform and document bias testing and oversight. Employer remains liable even when the tool is built or operated by a third-party vendor.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: LAD remedies: compensatory and punitive damages, statutory penalties up to $10,000-$50,000 per tier, attorneys' fees.

compliance_rules.regulation: Candidate PII Protection & Data Governance (GDPR / CCPA / DPDP / SOC 2)
compliance_rules.scope: Storage, transmission, and vendor processing of all candidate personal data across TalentMesh, including biometric and special-category data.
compliance_rules.requirement: Encrypt candidate PII in transit and at rest, enforce least-privilege access, log access, require SOC 2 Type II (or equivalent) from AI/data vendors, and apply data minimization and purpose limitation to every candidate record.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Cross-regime exposure: GDPR up to 4% of turnover; DPDP up to INR 250 crore; CCPA per-violation fines; BIPA statutory damages.

compliance_rules.regulation: Candidate Data Retention & Deletion
compliance_rules.scope: Lifecycle retention and deletion of candidate records, interview videos, biometric data, and AI outputs across all jurisdictions.
compliance_rules.requirement: Apply a documented retention schedule: honor candidate deletion requests (interview videos deleted within 30 days per IL AIVA); retain AI selection records for the longer of 4 years (CA FEHA / UGESP) or the local statutory period; purge biometric data per the BIPA destruction policy; never retain data beyond its stated purpose.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Over-retention triggers GDPR/CCPA/BIPA exposure; under-retention undermines the adverse-impact defense.

compliance_rules.regulation: Candidate Consent & Notice for AI Use
compliance_rules.scope: Point-of-collection consent and AI-use notice across the recruiter screen, assessment, video interview, and screening-chatbot stages.
compliance_rules.requirement: Before any AI evaluation, present a clear AI-use notice and capture affirmative consent where required (IL AIVA video analysis, BIPA biometrics, MD facial recognition); record the consent artifact with timestamp and version, and provide an alternative process if consent is declined.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Missing consent triggers BIPA statutory damages, IL AIVA/MD violations, and GDPR consent-basis failure.

compliance_rules.regulation: Human-in-the-Loop Approval & Oversight Logging
compliance_rules.scope: Every AI-assisted advancement or rejection decision at each stage of the hiring funnel.
compliance_rules.requirement: Require a named, competent human to approve every candidate advancement or rejection that AI assisted; log the AI recommendation, the human reviewer's identity, the decision, and the rationale. Retain oversight logs at least 6 months (EU AI Act) and surface them on candidate explanation requests.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Absence of human oversight breaches EU AI Act, GDPR Art. 22, and state ADMT rights regimes simultaneously.

compliance_rules.regulation: Right to Explanation of Individual AI Decisions
compliance_rules.scope: Candidate-initiated requests to understand an AI-assisted hiring outcome (EU AI Act Art. 86, GDPR Arts. 13-15, state ADMT frameworks).
compliance_rules.requirement: On request, provide an affected candidate a meaningful, plain-language explanation of the main factors and logic behind an AI-assisted decision and the available avenues to contest it or obtain human review, within the jurisdiction's response window.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Failure breaches EU AI Act Art. 86, GDPR transparency duties, and Colorado/California ADMT rights regimes.

compliance_rules.regulation: Canada — Ontario Bill 149 & Quebec Law 25 (AI Hiring Disclosure & Automated Decisions)
compliance_rules.scope: Hiring of Canadian candidates: Ontario publicly advertised job postings and Quebec automated-decision processing.
compliance_rules.requirement: Disclose in publicly advertised Ontario job postings whether AI is used to screen, assess, or select applicants; for Quebec, inform candidates of decisions based exclusively on automated processing and provide the right to know the personal information and principal factors used, to correct it, and to submit observations.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Quebec Law 25 administrative penalties up to CAD $10M or 2% of worldwide turnover; Ontario ESA penalties.

compliance_rules.regulation: UK Equality Act 2010 & UK GDPR (Data (Use and Access) Act 2025)
compliance_rules.scope: Hiring of UK candidates using AI; covers direct/indirect discrimination and automated-decision transparency.
compliance_rules.requirement: Ensure AI does not cause direct or indirect discrimination against protected characteristics, make reasonable adjustments for disabled candidates in AI assessments, comply with UK GDPR automated-decision and transparency rules, and complete a DPIA for high-risk AI processing per ICO guidance.
compliance_rules.penalty_note: Uncapped employment-tribunal compensation for discrimination; ICO fines up to GBP 17.5M or 4% of global turnover.

frameworks.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 + Generative AI Profile (NIST AI 600-1)
frameworks.relevance: De facto US standard of care for hiring-AI governance, organized around Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage. Adoption underpins the TRAIGA safe harbor and strengthens defensibility under disparate-impact and state ADMT regimes; the GenAI Profile guides LLM/chatbot supply-chain risk for AI recruiter and screening tools.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System (AIMS)
frameworks.relevance: Certifiable AI management system standard providing the governance wrapper for the hiring-AI program: AI policy, leadership accountability, AI impact assessments, lifecycle and supplier controls, and Annex A controls for fairness, transparency, and human oversight. Certification evidences systematic risk/impact assessment supporting EU AI Act and vendor due-diligence expectations.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI Risk Management Guidance
frameworks.relevance: Applies ISO 31000 risk principles to AI-specific risks (bias, robustness, explainability, data quality), operationalizing the risk-assessment requirements of ISO 42001 and the EU AI Act FRIA. Supplies the detailed methodology for identifying, analyzing, and treating bias and validity risk in hiring tools.

frameworks.name: AICPA SOC 2 (Trust Services Criteria) — Type II
frameworks.relevance: Independent attestation over security, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy controls for systems processing candidate personal and biometric data. A SOC 2 Type II report from hiring-AI vendors is the standard procurement and due-diligence control underpinning GDPR/DPDP/CCPA/BIPA data-governance obligations.

data_subject_rights.name: Right of Access to Candidate Data
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Candidates submit a verified request via the careers portal; Scribe assembles the personal data held, its sources, and processing purposes and returns it within the statutory window (30 days GDPR/UK GDPR, 45 days CCPA).

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Deletion / Erasure
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Deletion requests trigger Scribe to purge candidate records subject to legal-retention holds; AI interview videos are deleted within 30 days (IL AIVA) and biometric data per the BIPA destruction policy, with recipients instructed to delete shared copies.

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Explanation of an AI Decision
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Echo returns a plain-language explanation of the principal factors and logic behind the AI-assisted decision, drawn from the oversight log captured under COMP-HUMAN-01, plus the avenues to contest or seek human review.

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Human Review / Intervention
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Candidates may contest an automated outcome; the case is routed to a named human reviewer who re-evaluates without sole reliance on the AI, with the intervention recorded per GDPR Art. 22 and the EU AI Act.

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Opt Out / Alternative Process
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Echo offers a non-AI alternative selection path on request (NYC LL144, ADA, CCPA/CPRA ADMT opt-out); the candidate proceeds through human-driven evaluation with no penalty and the opt-out is logged.

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Correction of Personal Data
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Candidates may correct inaccurate personal data and submit observations on automated decisions (Quebec Law 25); Scribe updates the record and re-runs affected evaluations where appropriate.

data_subject_rights.name: Right to Notice of AI Use
data_subject_rights.how_supported: Echo presents an AI-use notice at the point of collection and in job postings (Ontario Bill 149, IL HB3773, NYC LL144 10-business-day notice), describing what the AI assesses and the data collected before any AI evaluation occurs.
